Goodbaby International Holdings ( (HK:1086) ) has shared an update.

Goodbaby International Holdings reported full-year 2025 revenue of HK$8.66 billion, down 1.2% year-on-year, as modestly lower sales and stable cost of sales led to a 1.6% decline in gross profit to HK$4.43 billion. Operating profit fell 16.1% to HK$419.5 million and profit attributable to shareholders dropped 38.6% to HK$218.6 million, with basic and diluted earnings per share sliding to HK$0.13 from HK$0.21, highlighting weaker profitability despite largely steady top-line performance and indicating margin pressure and higher tax charges for investors to monitor.

Goodbaby International Holdings is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands that operates in the juvenile and baby products industry. The group designs, manufactures and sells childcare products, with revenues primarily generated through the sale of its brands and products to global markets.

data.description:

The latest update is out from Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited ( (HK:3692) ).

Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited has proposed a final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.20 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, reflecting the board’s decision to return cash to shareholders following its annual results. Subject to shareholder approval, the stock will trade ex-dividend on 3 July 2026, with the record date set for 9 July 2026 and payment scheduled for 24 July 2026, providing income visibility and a defined timetable for investors tracking the company’s capital return policy.

The dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars through the company’s share registrar, Tricor Investor Services Limited, with no withholding tax or adjustments for exchange rate disclosed in the notice. The announcement offers clarity on key dates and amounts for shareholders and signals management’s continued commitment to cash distributions, which may be seen as an indicator of confidence in Hansoh Pharmaceutical’s financial position and future cash generation.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3692) stock is a Buy with a HK$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited stock, see the HK:3692 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited

Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed pharmaceutical company focused on developing, manufacturing and marketing prescription drugs. The group primarily serves the healthcare market with a portfolio of medicines targeting major therapeutic areas in China and potentially other markets, positioning itself as a key player among regional pharma issuers on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

data.title: Hansoh Pharma Delivers Double-Digit 2025 Growth as Innovative Drugs Drive Earnings and Pipeline Wins
data.description:

An announcement from Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited ( (HK:3692) ) is now available.

Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group reported strong 2025 results, with revenue rising 22.6% to RMB15.03 billion and profit up 27.1% to RMB5.56 billion, driven by a 30.4% surge in innovative and collaborative medicines, which now account for 82.2% of sales. The board proposed a higher full-year dividend after earnings per share climbed 26.4%, reflecting management’s confidence in cash generation and the sustainability of its innovation-led model.

Operationally, Hansoh advanced a series of key pipeline assets, including its self-developed B7-H3-targeted ADC Risvutatug Rezetecan gaining Breakthrough Therapy status from both U.S. and Chinese regulators for relapsed or refractory osteosarcoma. The company also secured NMPA approval for a second indication of its biologic XINYUE in IgG4-related disease and expanded clinical programs in oncology and immunology, moves that strengthen its positioning in high-value specialty segments and support future growth visibility.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3692) stock is a Buy with a HK$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited stock, see the HK:3692 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited

Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group is a leading innovation-driven drugmaker in mainland China, focused on major disease areas including oncology, anti-infectives, central nervous system, metabolism and immunology. The company has launched seven innovative medicines that generate sales in China, and its portfolio of innovative and collaborative products now underpins the bulk of its revenue and long-term growth strategy.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo ( (HK:3696) ) has provided an update.

InSilico Medicine reported major upgrades to its Pharma.AI platform, enhancing Biology42, Chemistry42, and Science42 with new target discovery metrics, generative biologics capabilities, and multimodal foundation models such as Nach01. These advances aim to move the company closer to pharmaceutical superintelligence and expand its role in AI-enabled drug discovery workflows.

The company also advanced a broad therapeutic pipeline, adding six AI-designed preclinical candidates to reach a total of 28 and achieving key clinical milestones across multiple programs. Lead asset ISM001-055 for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis delivered promising Phase IIa data, while several oncology and inflammatory disease candidates entered early-stage global trials, strengthening InSilico’s future out-licensing and partnering potential.

More about InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is an AI-driven biotechnology company focused on drug discovery and development. Its Pharma.AI platform integrates biology, chemistry, and scientific reasoning tools to design novel therapeutics across fibrosis, oncology, immunology, metabolic disorders, pain, and other disease areas, targeting partnerships and out-licensing opportunities in global pharma markets.

data.title: Starcoin Group Plans Debt-Equity Deal to Cut Liabilities and Add Strategic Investor
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech ( (HK:0399) ) has shared an update.

Starcoin Group Limited has signed a memorandum of understanding with Hong Kong-incorporated Marvel Nice Limited for a possible share subscription, following Marvel Nice’s intended acquisition of HK$715 million in convertible bonds previously issued by the company. Under the non-binding terms, Marvel Nice would subscribe for new shares at no less than HK$1 per share, with the subscription price to be settled by offsetting the outstanding principal and interest of the bonds.

If completed, the transaction would substantially reduce Starcoin’s liabilities, bring in a new strategic shareholder and bolster its financial position for ongoing development of its beauty products trading and oral insulin businesses. The board believes the potential deal would broaden the company’s shareholder and capital base, provide additional funding capacity and create room for future business development opportunities, positioning the group for more diversified growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0399) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.12 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech stock, see the HK:0399 Stock Forecast page.

More about Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech

Starcoin Group Limited, formerly Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech Limited, is an investment holding company whose group operations focus on trading beauty products in Hong Kong and on the research, development and commercialisation of an oral insulin product. The company is seeking to diversify risk, broaden income sources and improve cash reserves while strengthening its shareholder and capital base.

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Greenland Hong Kong Holdings ( (HK:0337) ) is now available.

Greenland Hong Kong Holdings reported a sharp decline in profitability for 2025, as revenue fell to RMB 12.32 billion from RMB 15.28 billion and gross profit contracted to RMB 201.17 million from RMB 796.30 million. The deterioration reflects continued pressure on its real estate operations, with weaker sales and margins amid a challenging property market.

The group’s net loss widened to RMB 2.51 billion from RMB 2.29 billion, driven by higher income tax expenses, significant impairment losses under the expected credit loss model, and a further loss in the fair value of investment properties. Additional drag came from elevated finance costs and other operating expenses, underscoring ongoing balance sheet and market headwinds for shareholders and creditors despite modest gains in joint ventures and investment valuations.

More about Greenland Hong Kong Holdings

Greenland Hong Kong Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates primarily in property development and related real estate businesses. An update from Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1799) ) is now available.

Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. operates in the energy industry and is subject to both PRC company law and Hong Kong Main Board Listing Rules, indicating a dual regulatory environment. The firm’s governance framework relies on a board-level nomination committee that manages the selection and review of directors and senior executives.

The company has formalized detailed terms of reference for its nomination committee, which will set selection criteria, vet candidates, and report to the board. The committee must have five directors, a majority of whom are independent non-executive directors and at least one of a different gender, underscoring a stronger commitment to independence, diversity, and structured corporate governance processes.

Members and the chair of the nomination committee are appointed through defined board procedures, and their terms align with those of directors, ensuring continuity and accountability. These measures are intended to improve board composition, standardize nomination management, and enhance oversight, which may bolster investor confidence and align the company with prevailing governance standards in Hong Kong’s capital markets.

Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1799) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. has established an Audit Committee under its board of directors to strengthen internal supervision, risk control, and corporate governance in line with PRC company law, Hong Kong Listing Rules, and its own articles of association. The committee will review financial information and disclosure, oversee internal and external audit work, and supervise internal controls, supported by the company’s audit department, which will handle preparatory and research tasks.

The Audit Committee will comprise five non-executive directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors and at least one member possessing professional accounting or financial management expertise, and it bars recent partners of the external audit firm from membership for one year to safeguard independence. These measures are designed to enhance the integrity of financial oversight, improve transparency, and reinforce investor confidence in the company’s governance framework.

CMGE Technology Group Limited ( (HK:0302) ) has shared an announcement.

CMGE Technology Group reported 2025 revenue of RMB1.39 billion, down from the prior year, and a narrowed annual loss of RMB1.48 billion as its flagship cross-platform game Legend of Sword and Fairy: World underperformed expectations and weighed on production and operations. Despite the setback, the company improved operating results in the second half, driven by stricter cost discipline and a stronger performance from other titles.

Management accelerated overseas expansion, lifting overseas revenue 31.6% year-on-year to RMB358.2 million and raising its share to 25.8% of total sales as CMGE launched localized versions of multiple games across Japan, Greater China, Southeast Asia and Vietnam. In the Mainland China market, new IP-based releases such as Chunqiu Mystery, New Romance of the Three Kingdoms: The Legend of Cao Cao and Naruto: Konoha Masters exceeded or met expectations and contributed incremental revenue, underscoring the firm’s strategy to leverage proprietary and licensed IP to stabilize growth after the flagship miss.

data.title: InSilico Medicine Strikes Multi-Billion-Dollar AI Drug Discovery Deal With Eli Lilly
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo ( (HK:3696) ) has issued an update.

InSilico Medicine has entered a global pipeline licensing and AI drug discovery collaboration with Eli Lilly, leveraging its Pharma.AI engine to accelerate the discovery and development of novel oral therapeutics across multiple disease areas. Lilly receives an exclusive worldwide license to develop, manufacture and commercialize certain preclinical oral therapies, while the partners jointly run R&D programs on Lilly-selected targets, combining Insilico’s AI platforms with Lilly’s development and disease expertise.

Under the agreement, Insilico is entitled to a USD115 million upfront payment and could earn up to approximately USD2.75 billion in development, regulatory and commercial milestones, plus tiered royalties on future sales. The deal significantly enhances Insilico’s financial resources and validates its AI-driven drug discovery model, strengthening the company’s position as a key AI partner for large pharmaceutical groups and potentially accelerating the translation of its pipeline into commercially viable therapies.

More about InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is a Hong Kong-listed biotechnology company that integrates artificial intelligence and automation to accelerate drug discovery and advance life sciences. Its in-house capabilities and Pharma.AI platforms target unmet medical needs in fibrosis, oncology, immunology, pain, obesity and metabolic disorders, while also extending into advanced materials, agriculture, nutrition and veterinary medicine.

Top Spring International Holdings Limited ( (HK:3688) ) has provided an update.

Top Spring International Holdings Limited has warned that its net loss for the year ended 31 December 2025 is expected to widen to about HK$3.0 billion, compared with a net loss of approximately HK$1.9 billion a year earlier. The board highlighted that the deterioration stems mainly from fair value losses on investment properties, fair value losses on certain financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss, and impairment losses on certain inventories.

The figures are based on unaudited consolidated management accounts and a preliminary board assessment, and may differ from the final audited annual results due to ongoing finalisation and review. The company plans to publish its audited annual results on 30 March 2026 and has cautioned shareholders and potential investors to exercise care and seek professional advice when dealing in its securities, underscoring heightened risk and uncertainty around its financial position.

data.title: China Financial Services Holdings Boosts Profit on Reversals Despite Lower Financing Income
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Financial Services Holdings Limited ( (HK:0605) ) has provided an update.

China Financial Services Holdings Limited reported a 7.9% decline in interest and financing consultancy services income to HK$93.6 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, but overall profitability improved sharply. Profit attributable to owners rose 73.3% to HK$71.5 million, and basic earnings per share increased to HK$0.35, driven largely by reversals of loan and interest payables and lower impairment losses compared with the prior year.

Despite higher interest and handling expenses and reduced core financing income, the Group benefited from HK$86.97 million in reversals of loan and interest payables and smaller write-downs on financial instruments, which offset general and administrative costs. The emergence of HK$10.2 million in education service income also contributed to the top line, suggesting an evolving business mix that may reduce reliance on its traditional financing consultancy segment and potentially support more resilient earnings going forward.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0605) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.86 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Financial Services Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0605 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Financial Services Holdings Limited

China Financial Services Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, operating primarily in interest and financing consultancy services. The group has also begun generating income from education services, indicating a move to diversify its revenue streams beyond traditional financing-related activities.
